name: El Garduño de Cela Trail
name_original: El Garduño de Cela — circular por Durón, Budia y el Olivar
description: Hike the moderate 7.2 miles (11.7 km) El Garduño de Cela Trail through Alcarria's literary landscapes and charming villages.
region_intro: Hiking around Durón offers routes through varied landscapes, often featuring the shores of reservoirs and river paths. The region is characterized by rolling hills and open terrain, with some trails passing through agricultural areas and small villages. Elevation changes are generally moderate, providing accessible options for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Durón The most popular hiking route is El Garduño de Cela Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 25 minutes to complete, featuring moderate ascents and descents. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja, an easy 1.5 miles (2.5 km) path. This short circular walk offers views of the reservoir and is suitable for beginner hiking routes Durón. Local hikers also love the On the shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Mantiel along the Tajo Natural Path, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes and along the Tajo Natural Path, often completed in about 6 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Durón is defined by reservoir shorelines, river paths, and open countryside, with options ranging from easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including family-friendly hiking Durón. The routes in Durón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 7 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Durón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Durón?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;5&lt;/strong&gt; distinct hiking routes documented around Durón on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Durón?
A: Yes, Durón offers several easy routes perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt;, which is a short 1.5-mile (2.5 km) path offering pleasant views of the reservoir. Another accessible choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alocén loop from Alocén&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) trail.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Durón?
A: Hikes in Durón vary in length and duration. You can find shorter, easy walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 1.5 miles (2.5 km) and takes less than an hour. For longer excursions, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022641343/on-the-shores-of-the-entrepenas-reservoir-circular-from-mantiel-along-the-tajo-natural-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Mantiel along the Tajo Natural Path&lt;/a&gt; spans 13.4 miles (21.6 km) and can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Durón?
A: Yes, the region around Durón provides routes suitable for families. The varied terrain, with moderate elevation changes and reservoir shorelines, offers accessible options. Shorter, easier loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; are particularly well-suited for families with children.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Durón?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Durón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dique de Pareja – Azud de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022641343/on-the-shores-of-the-entrepenas-reservoir-circular-from-mantiel-along-the-tajo-natural-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Mantiel along the Tajo Natural Path&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails around Durón?
A: The trails often feature the scenic shores of the Entrepeñas Reservoir and river paths. You can also discover natural landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6689105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana (1,145 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Redonda (1,144 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The area also includes cultural points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4313970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3764777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Durón?
A: Durón's climate, characterized by rolling hills and open terrain, makes it enjoyable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, but reservoir-side trails provide refreshing views. Winter hiking is possible, though some trails might be more exposed to the elements.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Durón?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, many of the open countryside and reservoir-side paths around Durón are generally suitable for dog walking. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or wildlife, and to carry water for them.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Durón?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes starting from villages like Pareja, Mantiel, Alocén, or Budia will likely have designated parking areas or roadside parking options within the village. It's advisable to check specific starting points on komoot for more detailed parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Durón?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Durón, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.9 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, particularly the scenic reservoir shorelines and the tranquility of the open countryside. The well-maintained paths and the variety of routes for different skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' trails to explore in Durón?
A: Beyond the most popular routes, exploring trails that connect smaller villages like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Budia – Durón loop from Budia&lt;/a&gt; can offer a more secluded experience. These routes often pass through agricultural areas and offer a glimpse into the local rural life, away from more frequented paths.
tips: The town is surrounded by four hermitages that we can also visit, dedicated to Santa Bárbara, La Soledad, San Roque and Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza, built in 1691 by Juan García Ochaíta. The latter stands out for its large dimensions and for having been moved stone by stone to the top of a hill due to works on the reservoir.

The town sits on the right bank of the Tagus River and belongs to the Comarca de la Alcarria. In the background we have the beautiful Entrepeñas reservoir that gives it some unique places. This land is rich in the production of oil and in its center the Plaza Mayor and the church of La Asunción stand out.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on the El Garduño de Cela Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered to have a moderate difficulty level. It spans approximately 11.7 km with an elevation gain of about 330 meters. You'll encounter a mix of well-maintained paths, tracks, and some single-track sections. Expect varied terrain, including walking through a humid poplar grove near Durón, descents through cereal fields, and ancient olive groves. While generally in good condition, some sections might have slippery stones or overgrown vegetation.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail?
faqs.answer: At a leisurely pace, the trail usually takes about 3.5 to 4.5 hours to complete. This estimate does not include time spent exploring the charming villages or enjoying the viewpoints along the way.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the El Garduño de Cela Trail?
faqs.answer: The most pleasant weather for hiking this route is during spring and autumn. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, making for an ideal hiking experience.

faqs.question: Is the El Garduño de Cela Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and to carry water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the El Garduño de Cela Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting the villages of Durón, Budia, and El Olivar, you can typically find parking within any of these villages to begin your hike. Starting in Durón is a common option.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque views of the Tajo valley and the expansive Entrepeñas reservoir, particularly from a notable viewpoint in El Olivar. You'll also pass through the charming villages of Durón, Budia, and El Olivar, each with traditional architecture and historical sites like the Church of the Assumption of El Olivar. The diverse landscape includes poplar groves, savin forests, and dense pine and oak woodlands.

faqs.question: What is the literary significance of this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail holds deep literary significance as it retraces steps from Nobel laureate Camilo José Cela's renowned book, "Viaje a la Alcarria" (Journey to the Alcarria). The name "El Garduño de Cela" itself comes from a passage in his work where he described an encounter with a *garduño* (stone marten).

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, the trail can be suitable for families with older children who are accustomed to hiking. Younger children might find some sections challenging due to the distance and elevation changes. Consider the fitness level of all family members before embarking.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, since the trail passes through the villages of Durón, Budia, and El Olivar, you can find local cafes, restaurants, and possibly accommodation options within these settlements to refresh yourself before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the official trail named El garduño de Cela, covering approximately 94% of its path.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of El Olivar
